tif

Class IFDHandlerOld

  • java.lang.Object
    • tif.IFDHandlerOld
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ImageHandler, java.lang.AutoCloseable

    public class IFDHandlerOld
extends java.lang.Object
implements ImageHandler, java.lang.AutoCloseable
    Parses TIFF-based files (such as standard TIFF, EXIF in JPEGs, and DNG) by reading and interpreting Image File Directories (IFDs).

    Supports standard TIFF 6.0 parsing, including the primary IFD0 and linked sub-directories, such as EXIF, GPS, and INTEROP, which are traversed recursively via tag-defined pointers.

    Note: While this handler detects BigTIFF (version 43), it currently only supports Standard TIFF (version 42) parsing. This handler focuses on IFD structures, other metadata formats, such as XMP or ICCP, should be handled by the Image Parser.

    Since:
    5 September 2025
    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs
    See Also:
    TIFF 6.0 Specification

    • Constructor Detail

      • IFDHandlerOld

        public IFDHandlerOld(ByteStreamReader reader)
        Constructs a handler using the specified byte stream reader.
        Parameters:
        reader - the stream reader providing access to TIFF content

      • IFDHandlerOld

        public IFDHandlerOld(java.nio.file.Path fpath)
              throws java.io.IOException
        Constructs a handler that reads metadata from a file on disk.

        This constructor initialises a file-backed stream. To ensure the underlying file handle is released and to prevent file locks, this handler should be managed within a try-with-resources block.

        Parameters:
        fpath - the path to the image file
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if the file does not exist or is inaccessible

      • IFDHandlerOld

        public IFDHandlerOld(byte[] payload)
        Constructs a handler that reads metadata from an in-memory byte array.

        While this constructor does not open a file system resource, it is still recommended to use a try-with-resources block to maintain consistent resource management patterns and to ensure the reader is properly disposed of.

        Parameters:
        payload - byte array containing TIFF-formatted data

    • Method Detail

      • getDirectories

        public java.util.List<DirectoryIFD> getDirectories()
        Returns the list of IFD directories that were successfully parsed.
        Returns:
        an unmodifiable List of parsed DirectoryIFD structures

      • getTifByteOrder

        public java.nio.ByteOrder getTifByteOrder()
        Returns the byte order, indicating how metadata values will be interpreted correctly.
        Returns:
        either ByteOrder.BIG_ENDIAN or ByteOrder.LITTLE_ENDIAN

      • isBigTiffVersion

        public boolean isBigTiffVersion()
        Indicates whether the parsed file is a BigTIFF variant (version 43).
        Returns:
        true if the file is a BigTIFF variant

      • parseMetadata

        public boolean parseMetadata()
                      throws java.io.IOException
        Parses the image stream and populates the directory list, performing a deep scan of the IFD chain to collect all underlying data entries.

        In some cases (notably in JPEGs with EXIF data), where the initial directory identifier is IFD1, for example, storing a thumbnail image, a check is provided to swap with IFD0, which is the primary directory.

        If any part of the directory structure is found to be corrupt, the directory list is cleared to maintain data integrity.

        Specified by:
        parseMetadata in interface ImageHandler
        Returns:
        true if the IFD chain was successfully traversed and at least one directory was extracted
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs

      • close

        public void close()
           throws java.io.IOException
        Releases the file handle and closes the underlying stream reader.

        This is called automatically when using a try-with-resources block. Closing this handler ensures that any system locks on the file are released and memory resources are freed.

        Specified by:
        close in interface java.lang.AutoCloseable
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs while closing the reader

      • readTifHeader

        private long readTifHeader()
                    throws java.io.IOException
        Parses the TIFF header to identify byte order, version, and the initial IFD offset.

        Requirement: The stream must be positioned at the TIFF magic bytes. Any preambles, such as HEIF or JPEG markers, must be skipped prior to calling this method.

        Currently supports Standard TIFF (16-bit), but BigTIFF (64-bit) is detectable and unsupported. This process validates the magic bytes (II - 0x49 0x49 or MM - 0x4D 0x4D) and determines the offset to IFD0 based on the version detected. Anything that is not in compliance with the TIFF specification 6.0 or the file is malformed will fail-fast.

        Returns:
        the absolute offset to IFD0. Returns 0 if the header is malformed or if the version is unsupported, for example: BigTIFF
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs

      • navigateImageFileDirectory

        private boolean navigateImageFileDirectory(DirectoryIdentifier dirType,
                                           long startOffset)
                                    throws java.io.IOException
        Recursively traverses an IFD and its linked sub-directories.

        Iterates through entries and follows pointers to sub-IFDs, such as EXIF, GPS, or Interop. If a recursive call fails due to malformed data, it returns false to prevent processing a corrupt directory chain.

        Each IFD begins with a 2-byte entry count, followed by a sequence of 12-byte directory entries, and concludes with a 4-byte pointer to the next IFD.

        Parameters:
        dirType - the directory type being processed
        startOffset - the file offset where the IFD begins
        Returns:
        true if the directory and all linked IFDs were successfully parsed
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs

      • register

        private static <E extends java.lang.Enum<E> & Taggable> void register(java.lang.Class<E> enumClass,
                                                                      IFDHandlerOld.DirectoryCategory category)
        Registers all constants of each Taggable Enum class into the global registry.
        Type Parameters:
        E - an Enum type that implements Taggable
        Parameters:
        enumClass - the Class literal of the Enum, for example: TagIFD_Exif.class
        category - the logical directory category to which these tags belong

      • register2

        @Deprecated
private static void register2(Taggable[] tags,
                                           IFDHandlerOld.DirectoryCategory category)
        Deprecated. 
        Registers a collection of tag definitions into the global registry for a specific directory category.

        This method utilises a "First-Defined-Wins" policy via Map.putIfAbsent(K, V). If multiple enums (i.e., Baseline and Private) define the same Tag ID for the same category, the first one registered is retained and subsequent collisions are logged as errors. This ensures that standard TIFF specifications take precedence over vendor-specific extensions.

        Parameters:
        tags - an array of Taggable constants, typically from an Enum.values() call
        category - the logical IFDHandlerOld.DirectoryCategory where these tags are valid

      • getLogicalSchema

        private IFDHandlerOld.DirectoryCategory getLogicalSchema(DirectoryIdentifier dirType)
        Maps a physical DirectoryIdentifier to its corresponding logical IFDHandlerOld.DirectoryCategory to determine which tag schema should be used during parsing. *

        While TIFF files may contain multiple physical directories (IFD0, IFD1, etc.), they often share the same tag definitions (Baseline/Extension). Conversely, specialised sub-directories like EXIF or GPS require localised schemas where Tag IDs may overlap with those in the Root directory but carry different meanings.

        Parameters:
        dirType - the physical directory type identified during the file crawl
        Returns:
        the logical IFDHandlerOld.DirectoryCategory used to fetch the correct tag registry map
